catalysts_full.content:
# JPM — Catalysts

## TL;DR

Q1 2026 earnings (April 14) are fully priced — record $16.5B net income, EPS $5.94 vs $5.49 estimate (+8.2%), absorbed [run: 20260430_182725]. FOMC held at 3.50%–3.75% on April 29 — third consecutive hold, no surprise, neutral for NII stability [run: 20260430_182725]. The next scheduled binary event is **Q2 2026 earnings on July 14, 2026**, consensus EPS $5.36 [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30]. No actionable catalyst within the 10-trading-day swing window.

## Upcoming events

- **Q2 2026 earnings: July 14, 2026.** Consensus Q2 EPS $5.36. Full-year 2026 technology spend guided to $19.8B (+10% YoY). Next binary catalyst — 55+ trading days away [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **Iran/Strait of Hormuz:** US blockade of Iranian ports is ongoing. Oil prices elevated; Goldman raised Brent forecast to $90/bbl on blockade risk. IMF projects 19% energy commodity price increase in 2026 under conflict baseline. A Strait of Hormuz closure is the primary stop-thesis trigger for any JPM long [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **NII guidance watch:** Guidance trimmed from $104.5B to ~$103B in Q1 2026. Any further reduction is a negative catalyst. Next formal read at Q2 earnings July 14 [run: 20260430_182725].
- **Kevin Warsh Fed transition:** Powell stays as Board governor post-chair term; Warsh awaits Senate confirmation. Policy continuity preserved short-term but medium-term uncertainty remains [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].

## Recent news synthesis

- **Q1 2026 record earnings (Apr 14, fully priced):** Net income +13% YoY, EPS $5.94 beat $5.49 estimate, revenue $50.54B beat $49.17B, IB fees +18% YoY, record equities trading, AWM AUM $4.8T [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **Dimon credit-cycle warning (Apr 28):** CEO warned credit downturn will be "worse than people think" and flagged potential bond market issues — goes beyond his prior stagflation warning and reinforces macro caution [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **Tech budget: $19.8B in 2026 (+10% YoY):** CIO revealed budget targeting 65,000+ technologists; AI and infrastructure at the core — a long-term moat-widening investment [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **JPM raised S&P 500 target to 7,600:** Citing AI momentum and stronger earnings outlook [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **Apple Card acquisition:** $20B Goldman portfolio acquired, adding tech-savvy consumer accounts to Chase ecosystem [run: 20260430_051455].
- **Exec harassment lawsuit (Apr 30):** Senior JPM executive faces lawsuit alleging sexual harassment of junior banker — reputational risk headline, immaterial to fundamentals but negative sentiment [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].
- **Market context (Apr 30):** S&P 500 hit a new all-time high (+0.8%), Dow up 1.5% on Caterpillar earnings beat, Qualcomm +16%. Strong market day is broadly supportive for JPM price stability [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30].

## Insider activity

No recent insider trades in the Apr 30 data bundle [run: 20260430_182725]. Board directors bought shares in March 2026 — historical positive signal but activity has been quiet since [run: 20260415_093758].

| Average target | $339.46 | [web_research/JPM.json, 2026-04-30] |

## Last updated

2026-04-30. Catalyst data from web_research/JPM.json (run 20260430_182725). Supersedes prior entry from run 20260430_051455.

lessons_full.content:
## Format

Each lesson is one dated bullet:

`[DATE] | [TICKER] | [SETUP TYPE] | [OUTCOME: +$X / -$X] | [WHY it worked or failed in 1 sentence]`

The "why" should reference: the thesis at the time, the macro regime, which swing strategy recommended it.

---

## Patterns (auto-generated by Sunday compactor, last 30 days)

| Setup Type | Trades | Wins | Win Rate | Avg P&L | Total P&L |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | 1 | 0 | 0% | -$63.20 | -$63.20 |

## Lessons

2026-04-30 | NVDA | EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | -$63.20 (corrected from -$264.65) | Stop triggered at $205.30 (hourly Fib 61.8%); swing_trend_momentum and swing_breakout were bullish on AI capex thesis, but price breached the stop zone intraday amid stretched macro (RSI 81–87) with no bullish confirmation candle before exit. **Note:** trade was originally sized 67 shares ($14,020) due to a phantom $100K budget bug (fixed commit b2b472d). With the correct $25K budget at 14.2% vol-adj limit, position would have been 16 shares ($3,348). Corrected P&L reflects the strategy's actual signal quality, not the sizing error.
